title: Sysinternals
overview: Sysinternals is a collection of advanced Windows utilities (70+ tools) originally created by Mark Russinovich and Bryce Cogswell to help troubleshoot, analyze and manage Windows systems. The suite covers file/disk, networking, process, security, system-info and miscellaneous utilities used by sysadmins, developers and security professionals. (Microsoft Learn

study.overview: Sysinternals is a collection of advanced Windows utilities (70+ tools) originally created by Mark Russinovich and Bryce Cogswell to help troubleshoot, analyze and manage Windows systems. The suite covers file/disk, networking, process, security, system-info and miscellaneous utilities used by sysadmins, developers and security professionals. (Microsoft Learn
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.

importantCommands.code:
procdump -ma <PID> C:\dumps\process_$(pid).dmp
# Wait and capture on CPU spike:
procdump -c 90 -s 5 -ma <processname>
